properties.description: Largest and most famous classical garden in Suzhou — a UNESCO World Heritage Site dating to 1509
properties.sustainability_notes: UNESCO World Heritage Site; example of traditional Chinese sustainable garden design
properties.notes: Arrive at opening to avoid tour groups. Audio guide available in English.

properties.description: Suzhou's most iconic landmark — a 2,500-year-old hill topped by the Yunyan Pagoda (961 AD), which leans 3 degrees due to uneven foundations. Called the 'number one attraction under heaven' by Su Dongpo
properties.notes: The pagoda cannot be entered due to structural preservation, but the hill itself has multiple interesting sites including Sword Pool (剑池), the alleged burial site of the King of Wu. Arrive early. About 15 min by taxi from the garden cluster.

properties.description: Suzhou's best-preserved ancient canal street, following the same path it did 800 years ago — narrow stone-paved lanes running alongside a quiet canal with whitewashed houses, teahouses, and craft shops
properties.sustainability_notes: One of China's best examples of living historic urban fabric; residents still live alongside the canal
properties.notes: Free to walk. Start at the south end near the Suzhou Museum and walk north. Great for photography — the canal reflections are beautiful. A gondola ride on the canal (~100 CNY/person) is worth the splurge. Afternoon light is best for photos.

properties.description: World-class museum designed by architect I.M. Pei (his 'last gift to his hometown'), blending modernist geometry with traditional Suzhou garden aesthetics — the building itself is the main attraction
properties.sustainability_notes: Architecture references traditional Suzhou whitewash-and-tile aesthetic using modern sustainable materials
properties.notes: Free but timed-entry reservation required — book in advance online. The central courtyard with its abstract rockery inspired by Song Dynasty painting is stunning. Directly adjacent to the Humble Administrator's Garden — combine in the same morning.

properties.description: UNESCO World Heritage garden famous for its extraordinary labyrinthine rockery of limestone peaks resembling lions, built in 1342 by a Buddhist monk as a memorial to his teacher
properties.sustainability_notes: UNESCO World Heritage Site; one of four classic Suzhou gardens on the World Heritage list
properties.notes: Smaller and quicker to visit than Humble Administrator's Garden. The maze of caves and tunnels through the rockery is genuinely fun to explore. Combines well with the Suzhou Museum (a 5-minute walk). I.M. Pei visited as a child and incorporated its forms into his later work.
